explainability.path_to_improvement: Document how explanations are provided to users affected by AI decisions.

external_accountability.path_to_improvement: Obtain external validation of AI practices or require vendor certifications.

fairness.findings: An annual report mentions policy risks related to algorithmic bias.
fairness.path_to_improvement: Publish bias testing results, outcome monitoring, or vendor fairness certifications.

relevant_sources.summary: This Form 10-K annual report provides evidence for **fairness, governance, oversight, and privacy**. The report details the establishment of a "Global Artificial Intelligence Use and Development Policy" and a cross-functional AI steering committee responsible for reviewing and approving AI use cases, demonstrating strong **governance** and **oversight** mechanisms. Furthermore, the report mentions policy risks related to algorithmic bias and data protection, supporting the **fairness** and **privacy** pillars, respectively, and notes mandatory employee training on responsible AI use, indicating operational execution of governance.
relevant_sources.title: Form 10-K Annual Report for Fiscal Year 2025

governance.findings: An annual report notes mandatory employee training on responsible AI use. Sources reference the use of AI and machine learning for digital twins, suggesting governance over AI application and asset management. A structured approach to AI adoption is highlighted through the development of a playbook and advanced routing models.

relevant_sources.summary: This Form 10-K annual report provides evidence for **fairness, governance, oversight, and privacy**. The report details the establishment of a "Global Artificial Intelligence Use and Development Policy" and a cross-functional AI steering committee responsible for reviewing and approving AI use cases, demonstrating strong **governance** and **oversight** mechanisms. Furthermore, the report mentions policy risks related to algorithmic bias and data protection, supporting the **fairness** and **privacy** pillars, respectively, and notes mandatory employee training on responsible AI use, indicating operational execution of governance.
relevant_sources.title: Form 10-K Annual Report for Fiscal Year 2025

relevant_sources.summary: This article, "Three Ways AI is Impacting the Tower Industry," provides evidence for the **governance** and **transparency** pillars of responsible AI. The use of AI and machine learning for digital twins to analyze cell site data and reduce physical inspections suggests governance over AI application and asset management. Furthermore, the article implies transparency through the visualization and predictive capabilities of AI, as well as the establishment of ML operations for performance enhancement.
relevant_sources.title: Three Ways AI is Impacting the Tower Industry

relevant_sources.summary: This blog post from WIA describes American Tower's use of AI and analytics for network operations, providing evidence for **governance, oversight, and transparency**. The post highlights a structured approach to AI adoption through the development of a playbook and advanced routing models, indicating a commitment to governance. Furthermore, the mention of rules-based analytics and automated anomaly flagging before human review demonstrates mechanisms for oversight, while discussions of future AI adoption phases and decision-making frameworks suggest a strategic plan for transparency.
relevant_sources.title: AI and Analytics at the Core: American Tower Reimagining Network Operations

oversight.findings: Sources reference rules-based analytics and automated anomaly flagging before human review as mechanisms for oversight.

privacy.findings: An annual report mentions policy risks related to data protection. A company blog post mentions data protection measures, including the absence of personal data and the use of encryption in automated bot management, indicating a policy for handling data responsibly.
privacy.path_to_improvement: Publish AI-specific privacy impact assessments or data handling procedures.

summary.overall_findings: American Tower's public disclosures include both operational practices and policy-level statements across its responsible AI pillars. Operational evidence describes rules-based analytics and automated anomaly flagging as mechanisms for oversight, alongside mandatory employee training on responsible AI use for governance. Policy-level documentation addresses transparency through the visualization and predictive capabilities of AI, as well as fairness and privacy, with specific data protection measures noted for automated bot management. In total, 5 of 7 evaluated pillars have documented evidence, drawing from 7 publicly available sources. No qualifying public evidence was found for explainability or external accountability.
